segments.text: And the famine was sore in the land.
And it came to pass, when they had eaten up the corn which they had brought out of Egypt,
Go again, buy us a little food.
segments.speaker: Israel

segments.text: And Judah spake unto him, saying,
The man did solemnly protest unto us, saying, Ye shall not see my face, except your brother be with you.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: If thou wilt send our brother with us, we will go down and buy thee food:
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: But if thou wilt not send him, we will not go down: for the man said unto us, Ye shall not see my face, except your brother be with you.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: Wherefore dealt ye so ill with me, as to tell the man whether ye had yet a brother?
segments.speaker: Israel

segments.text: The man asked us straitly of our state, and of our kindred, saying, Is your father yet alive? have ye another brother? and we told him according to the tenor of these words: could we certainly know that he would say, Bring your brother down?
segments.speaker: Brothers

footnotes: could…: Heb. knowing could we know

segments.text: And Judah said unto Israel his father,
Send the lad with me, and we will arise and go; that we may live, and not die, both we, and thou, and also our little ones.
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: I will be surety for him; of my hand shalt thou require him: if I bring him not unto thee, and set him before thee, then let me bear the blame for ever:
segments.speaker: Judah

segments.text: For except we had lingered, surely now we had returned this second time.
And their father Israel said unto them,
If it must be so now, do this; take of the best fruits in the land in your vessels, and carry down the man a present, a little balm, and a little honey, spices, and myrrh, nuts, and almonds:
segments.speaker: Israel

segments.text: And take double money in your hand; and the money that was brought again in the mouth of your sacks, carry it again in your hand; peradventure it was an oversight:
segments.speaker: Israel

segments.text: Take also your brother, and arise, go again unto the man:
segments.speaker: Israel

segments.text: And God Almighty give you mercy before the man, that he may send away your other brother, and Benjamin. If I be bereaved of my children, I am bereaved.
segments.speaker: Israel

footnotes: If…: or, And I, as I have been, etc.

segments.text: And the men took that present, and they took double money in their hand, and Benjamin; and rose up, and went down to Egypt, and stood before Joseph.
And when Joseph saw Benjamin with them, he said to the ruler of his house,
Bring these men home, and slay, and make ready; for these men shall dine with me at noon.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: And he asked them of their welfare, and said,
Is your father well, the old man of whom ye spake? Is he yet alive?
segments.speaker: Joseph

footnotes: Is your…: Heb. Is there peace to your father?

segments.text: And he lifted up his eyes, and saw his brother Benjamin, his mother's son, and said,
Is this your younger brother, of whom ye spake unto me?
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: God be gracious unto thee, my son.
segments.speaker: Joseph

segments.text: And he took and sent messes unto them from before him: but Benjamin's mess was five times so much as any of theirs.
And they drank, and were merry with him.
